                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | full (planned)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Status / artifacts / blockers / suggested next persona = the message; the next persona is the continuation. Error budget                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Beyer et al., *Site Reliability Engineering*, 2016, Ch. 3                                                                                                                                                      | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Tokens treated as the budget you spend; `claude-budget-guard` is the SRE-style burn-rate alerting.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Havelund & Goldberg, "Verify Your Runs", *VSTTE* 2008                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | partial                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Per rule #10, the deterministic share lives in `scripts/check-rule-{1..7}-*.mjs` + `scripts/check-pattern-index.mjs` + `scripts/check-pr-self-grade.mjs` (each a load-bearing CI gate). The residual judgement share lives in `novel/spec-monitor/` as an advisory-only Claude Skill (row 35) capped at ≤5 advisory rules. Original "full runtime verification" framing is now declared a deviation: classical runtime verifiers produce deterministic verdicts uniformly; here the verdict is split by mechanisability, with "what cannot be deterministically checked" explicitly carved out per rule #10.                                                                                                                                                      | partial                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Single-file JSON ledger; `mkdir`-based atomic lock. **Deviation:** not durable across machine death (no fsync barriers; no replication). **Why acceptable:** single-machine bootstrap; if the laptop dies mid-setup, `--reset` is the recovery path. **What would restore full conformance:** SQLite + WAL mode for the ledger — over-engineered today, on the table when the multi-machine task lands.                                                                                                                                                      | partial                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Discrete priority levels (P0–P3) instead of numerical; preemption is at task boundaries (cooperative), not interrupt-level. **Why acceptable:** task durations are minutes-to-hours, so cooperative scheduling has no observable cost; discrete levels match human triage.                  | Time-bounded exclusive access. **Deviation:** no explicit timeout; staleness is detected by the picker rule "Found + stale (no related code) → unclaim". **Why acceptable:** humans + git history are the timeout signal.                  | A living catalogue indexed by artifact, anchored in source, with explicit conformance level — the form pattern languages take in software architecture per Gabriel. | 23  | Constitutional rule #9 + the per-task measurement contract                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Goal-Question-Metric + pre-registration                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Basili, Caldiera, Rombach, "The Goal-Question-Metric Approach", *Encyclopedia of Software Engineering* 1994; Munafò et al., "A Manifesto for Reproducible Science", *Nature Human Behaviour* 1, 0021, 2017 (pre-registration); Fagerholm et al., "Building Blocks for Continuous Experimentation", *RCoSE* 2014                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Goal = success criterion. Question = "what would tell us the goal is being met?". Metric = the OTEL query / shell command in the measurement-method cell. Pre-registration (committing the prediction before observing) is what makes the experiment falsifiable; pivot threshold operationalises Ries 2011's pivot-or-persevere. Iron rule: no exemption for bugfixes — preparation PRs land the metric source first when missing.                                                                                                                                                                                   |

deviation declared" section.                      | Diff-based: every newly-added (status A) file under `novel/**`, root `*.md`, `setup.sh`, `distribution/**`, or `.github/workflows/**` must be mentioned by `vision.md` § "Pattern conformance index" (full path, package-prefix, or basename), unless the file's first ~20 lines carry `<!-- pattern: not-applicable — <reason> -->` (≥3-char reason). Test files (`*.test.ts`, `*.test.mjs`), fixture files, and node_modules are skipped. Modifications/renames/deletions are skipped — same grandfathering precedent as rule-1 / rule-3 / rule-4. Pure function `checkPatternIndex({ changedFiles, visionMdContent, optOuts })` is the load-bearing contract; CLI is the I/O boundary. `scripts/check-pr-self-grade.mjs` carry the load-bearing share. The Skill addresses ≤5 residual judgement-heavy concerns (A1 hypothesis vagueness, A5 conformance level mismatched to source — A2 pivot equals success was promoted to deterministic CI lint at row 39, A4 measurement output unchecked at row 40, A3 non-primary anchor at row 52, all retired from the Skill); growing past 5 requires either retiring one or shipping a deterministic linter (rule-#10 ratchet). **Deviation from Havelund-Goldberg 2008:** classical runtime verification produces deterministic verdicts; this Skill produces advisory verdicts only. The deterministic share of runtime verification lives in the linters above; this row covers the residual *judgement* share that rule #10 explicitly carves out as "what cannot be deterministically checked is not a constitutional rule" — kept as an advisory companion, not an enforcement mechanism.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Closes the weekly–monthly layer of rule #9. Daily 09:00 UTC cron walks `experiment-store/*.jsonl`, finds the merge boundary, and re-runs the recorded `measurement` for any replay window whose boundary has elapsed. Pure function `replayExperiment(...)` is referentially transparent over `{ meta, record, priorReplays, currentValueStdout, now, ref, windowDays }`; the CLI is the I/O boundary. Verdict ladder: `validated` writes one line to `validated-learnings.md`; `regressed` (only after two consecutive pivot crossings — matches Risk mitigation) appends a `pivot-experiment-<id>` task to TASKS.md; `inconclusive` is the default. Corrupt JSONL surfaces as a warning, never crashes the run (rule #7).                                                                                                                                                          | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Closes the "Integration test (deferred)" clause in `distribution/README.md`. Exercises rows 1–4 of the failure-mode table empirically: SIGKILLs `minsky-tick-loop` and asserts respawn within 10 s (row 1), kills `minsky-budget-guard` and asserts permanent restart (row 3), verifies graceful stop does not respawn (row 2 weak form), runs a kill storm and asserts blast-radius containment (row 4). Linux mode bootstraps systemd `--user` units with a `loginctl enable-linger` + `dbus-run-session` fallback chain; macOS mode bootstraps LaunchAgents into `gui/$(id -u)`. Driver renders the templates' stub `run-*.sh` runners on the fly because the real tick-loop / budget-guard implementations land in follow-up tasks — restart-policy verification only depends on process presence. **Caveat:** GH-hosted Ubuntu runners' user-systemd plumbing is fragile; the driver exits 77 (skipped) if no user-bus is reachable, and the CI gate accepts `skipped` to preserve the merge path while still surfacing the signal when it runs. Pivot to a self-hosted runner is filed as `supervisor-integration-self-hosted-runner` in TASKS.md.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Pure decision function `checkPivotSuccessMargin({ success, pivot })` over the `@minsky/experiment-record`-parsed EXPERIMENT.yaml; CLI is the I/O boundary. Numeric extraction: scans each field for the first signed-numeric token (optional `-`/`+`, integer + optional fractional part). Decision table: both numeric → fail when `\|Δ\| / max(\|success\|, 1) < 1 %`; neither numeric and equal-after-trim/lower → fail (binary equality); neither numeric and differ → ok with advisory warning (long-tail prose stays advisory because the deterministic layer cannot read meaning); mixed → ok with advisory warning. Opt-out for legitimately-binary metrics: a top-level YAML comment line `# rule: ci-lint-pivot-success-margin: skip <reason>`. Pairs with `scripts/check-pivot-success-margin.test.mjs` (19 tests across the decision table). Ratchet: spec-monitor's `### A2.` heading is replaced by a tombstone `### A2 (retired …)` whose shape is intentionally outside the `check-skill-rule-cap` regex (rule #10's "remove the advisory rule when its deterministic counterpart ships" clause).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| 40  | `scripts/check-measurement-inspects-output.mjs` (CI lint promoting spec-monitor advisory rule A4 — measurement inspects output)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Deterministic CI gate over the rule-#9 `measurement` field; allowlist + blacklist token classifier over the parsed YAML record — rule-#10 ratchet applied to the spec-monitor Skill                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; ratchet rule: when a deterministic linter is added the matching advisory rule is removed in the same PR); Havelund & Goldberg, "Verify Your Runs", *VSTTE* 2008 (runtime verification — the inspect-output check is the deterministic-monitor layer carved out from the residual advisory share); Beck, *Extreme Programming Explained*, 1999 (CI as the constraint enforcer); `spec-advisories/2026-05-03-quarterly-audit.md` (audit decision: A4 admits a deterministic allowlist + blacklist)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Pure function `checkMeasurementInspectsOutput(measurementCmd) => { ok, level: "fail" \| "warn" \| "pass", reason }`; CLI is the I/O boundary (read EXPERIMENT.yaml, parse via `@minsky/experiment-record`, run the check, exit). Three-way verdict: `pass` (allowlist token present — `test`, `[`, `[[`, `jq -e`, `grep -q`/`-c`, `assert`, `vitest`, `pnpm test`/`typecheck`/`lint`, `@tasks-md/lint`, `markdownlint-cli2`, `gh run list ... --jq`, `node scripts/check-*.mjs`); `fail` (blacklist token present and no allowlist hit — `echo`, bare `true`, bare `curl`, bare `node`); `warn` (neither — residual judgement scope, exit 0 with stderr advisory). Word-boundary matching prevents `latest` from spuriously hitting `test`. Removed A4 from `novel/spec-monitor/SKILL.md` (the slot is reserved). Remote procedure call as the control surface, combined with cooperative cancellation via flag-file checkpoint (signals, graceful shutdown)                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Pause is delivered as an HTTPS request over Tailscale (RPC at the wire) but consumed as a flag-file rather than a synchronous RPC return — the in-flight tick checks the flag at the next safe point and self-suspends. Birrell-Nelson's transparency property is intentionally weakened: the caller does not block until the loop has paused; the Watch surface is the eventual-consistency read-back. Interface is the DSPy-fallback shape pre-registered in `research.md` § "DSPy fit" — the operationalisation of the rule-#2 invariant that vendor names not appear in business logic.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Pure function `checkAnchorPrimarySource(anchor) => { ok, level: "fail" \| "warn" \| "pass", reason }`; CLI is the I/O boundary (read EXPERIMENT.yaml, parse via `@minsky/experiment-record`, run the check, exit). Three-way verdict: `pass` (allowlist token present — italicised title `*…*`, `Ch. <n>`, `pp. <n>`, ISBN, DOI, `<VENUE> <YEAR>`, internal cross-ref `vision.md §` / `rule #<n>` / `spec-advisories/<date>.md`); `fail` (deny-list token present and no allowlist hit — `medium.com`, `*.substack.com`, `wikipedia.org`, `twitter.com`, `x.com`, `reddit.com`, `stackoverflow.com`, `chatgpt.com`, `claude.ai`, "ChatGPT said", "tweet by", "blog post"); `warn` (neither and anchor <25 chars — residual judgement scope, exit 0 with stderr advisory); long unrecognised prose passes silently. Allowlist wins over deny-list (a primary citation that contrasts against a Medium URL still passes). Opt-out: top-level YAML comment `# rule: ci-lint-anchor-primary-source: skip <reason ≥3 chars>`. Removed A3 from `novel/spec-monitor/SKILL.md` (the slot is reserved as a tombstone). | 54  | `novel/mape-k-loop/` (`@minsky/mape-k-loop` — pure `monitor(...)` + `analyze(...)` + `plan(...)` + `execute(...)` + `knowledge(...)` decision functions, `sustainedGain` + `oscillation` guards, and the assembled `tick(...)` cycle; all 4 sub-tasks of `mape-k-loop-v0` shipped)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | MAPE-K reference architecture (Monitor + Analyze + Plan + Execute + Knowledge phases) composed with Theory of Constraints (top constraint = max `violationCount × costEstimate`), pure-function decision module (Martin 2017 — every input is data, every output is data; the wrapping I/O is the user's), Kohavi-Tang-Xu sustained-gain window guard (default 7 d), Ries 2011 oscillation guard (default 10-iteration lookback), and a Helland 2007 immutable append-only log (`constraints.md`) as the Knowledge substrate                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Kephart & Chess, "The Vision of Autonomic Computing", *IEEE Computer* 36(1) 2003 (the MAPE-K loop — Monitor / Analyze / Plan / Execute / Knowledge); Goldratt, *The Goal*, North River Press, 1984 (Theory of Constraints — the constraint is whatever currently bottlenecks throughput); Martin, *Clean Architecture*, Pearson, 2017 (pure decision module + thin I/O boundary); Gregg, *Systems Performance*, Prentice Hall, 2014 (USE method — the per-rule aggregate-counter shape carries the *utilisation* column; saturation + errors deferred to a follow-up); Kohavi, Tang, Xu, *Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments*, Cambridge UP 2020, Ch. 3 (the sustained-gain window — 7 d default — operationalised by `sustainedGain`); Ries, *The Lean Startup*, Crown Business 2011 (build-measure-learn — the oscillation guard is the "don't re-pivot to a previously-rejected variant" guardrail); Helland, "Life beyond Distributed Transactions", *CIDR* 2007 (the append-only `constraints.md` Knowledge log); Munafò et al., "A Manifesto for Reproducible Science", *Nature Human Behaviour* 1, 0021, 2017 (the rule-#9 calibration-drift amendment that the Knowledge phase emits when MAE exceeds threshold); rule #1 README opt-out (existing autonomic-manager runtimes — IBM Tivoli, OpenStack Heat, Kubernetes operator-sdk — rejected because their decision modules couple to a specific cluster substrate; Minsky's input shape is markdown + YAML + GH Actions JSON)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | full — M+A+P+E+K phases + 2 guards + assembled `tick(...)` shipped; the user-story-003 integration test passes against the assembled package end-to-end                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Pure functions `monitor(input)`, `analyze({ snapshot, costs? })`, `plan({ topConstraint, evidence, basePrompt })`, `execute({ variants, evalSet, optimizer, metric, history, now, sustainedGainWindowDays?, oscillationLookback? })`, `knowledge({ verdictLog, calibrationDriftThreshold?, topConstraintRuleId, executeDecision, executeReason, winnerVariantId, now })`, and `tick({ … })` are referentially transparent — no I/O, no shared state, clocks injected via `now`. `tick` runs M → A → P → E → K and threads an optional `emit` seam so the wrapping CLI can carry OTEL spans (`mape.<phase>.<verb>`) without coupling the pure functions to `@minsky/observability`. `knowledge` writes a markdown block to append to `constraints.md` (every tick is logged — even no-ops; Helland 2007), and *conditionally* a `researchMdAmendmentProposal` text when the verdict-log MAE between predicted and observed Δ exceeds the `calibrationDriftThreshold` (default 50 % per Munafò et al. 2017 — pre-registration discipline). `monitor` aggregates parsed `CiRun` / `Advisory` / `ExperimentRecord` rows into a per-rule `HealthSnapshot`; corrupt rows are dropped with a `warnings` entry (graceful-degrade per rule #7) instead of throwing. `analyze` ranks rules by `violationCount × costEstimate(ruleId)`; ties are broken alphabetically by `ruleId` so output is deterministic across runs (matching the prompt-optimizer `pickWinner` tie-break in row 53). `plan` proposes ≤3 variants from a fixed v0 mutation catalogue (`enumerate-failure-modes`, `direct-answer`, `tighten-scope`); empty `ruleId` throws (let-it-crash on caller contract violation). `execute` runs the A/B via the `PromptOptimizer` adapter (row 53), then applies the two guards: `sustainedGain` returns `{ ok: false }` for cold starts, mid-window swaps, or windows shorter than the configured `windowDays`; `oscillation` blocks re-proposal of any variant that was `rejected` or `abstain`-ed within the last `lookbackIterations`. The `costs` argument is the seam where the `costEstimate` schedule sources per-rule severity weights from vision.md § "Cost schedule" (see that section for the v0 default — `rule-9 = 100`, `rule-7 = 50`, `rule-typo = 1`). `parseCostSchedule(visionMdContent)` (`novel/mape-k-loop/src/cost-schedule.ts`) is the upstream parser; the CLI wrapper threads its output into `tick({ ..., costs })` which forwards it to `analyze`. `costEstimate` is now full-conformance — the schedule is configurable, sourced from a literature-anchored constant in source per rule #4, and falls back to `DEFAULT_RULE_COST = 1` for any rule absent from the table (rule #7 graceful-degrade). Severity buckets (`low` / `medium` / `high`) thresholded at product ≥3 / ≥8 — conservative v0 cutoffs.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; ratchet rule: every prose-only invariant in a `novel/**/README.md` gets a deterministic linter as soon as the artefact it guards becomes machine-readable); `novel/mape-k-loop/constraints.md` preamble (the prose-only 200-entry archive cap); Helland, "Life beyond Distributed Transactions", *CIDR* 2007 (immutable log — derived data through reissue, not mutation; archive-split discipline); Beck, *Extreme Programming Explained*, 1999, Ch. 17 (CI as the constraint enforcer)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Pure function `checkConstraintsMdSize({ content, capEntries }) => { ok, count, reason? }`; CLI is the I/O boundary (read `novel/mape-k-loop/constraints.md`, run the check, exit). Counts lines matching `^## YYYY-MM-DD` (strict ISO-8601 date match — backticked prose mentions of the heading shape in the README preamble are not counted). Boundary semantics: the cap is *inclusive* — exactly 200 entries passes (Beyer SRE 2016 Ch. 3 — "you have used X % of your budget" is not a violation until X *exceeds* it; matches `check-mape-k-budget-cap`'s precedent). `capEntries` defaults to 200 (the README prose anchor); the constant is locked by a paired test so a silent edit surfaces loudly. Dormant state (rule #7 — graceful degrade): missing `constraints.md` exits 0 with a stderr advisory and re-arms the moment the file lands — same precedent as `check-skill-rule-cap.mjs`'s retired-Skill terminal state. Pivot (rule #9): if 200 proves arbitrary (the file is still readable at 400), raise the constant rather than removing the lint — removing it would re-open the unbounded-growth failure mode rule-#6 closes mechanically. Boundary semantics: the cap is *inclusive* — a config sitting at exactly 5.7 % passes (Beyer SRE 2016 Ch. 3 — "you have used X % of your budget" is not a violation until X *exceeds* it). Dormant state (rule #7 — graceful degrade): missing `config/mape-k.json` exits 0 with a stderr advisory and re-arms the moment the artefact lands — same precedent as `check-skill-rule-cap.mjs`'s retired-Skill terminal state. Pivot (rule #9): if the 5.7 % cap moves to a per-tier adaptive threshold (per `mape-k-loop`'s monthly self-calibration, research.md § "MAPE-K cadence"), retire this lint and replace with one that reads the calibrated value from the `mape-k-loop` Knowledge log.                                             | Each lint is a pure decision function over `{ config, expected }` returning `{ ok, reason? }`; CLI is the I/O boundary (read `config/<file>.json`, run the check, exit). Tick-loop reads `config/tick-loop.json`; the three MAPE-K-related lints all read `config/mape-k.json` (separate jobs, narrow assertions) — the cadence-pivot threshold colocates here per its TASKS.md "Files" brief, not in a separate `config/cadence.json`, because it is a property of the MAPE-K cadence. Each accepts two field-name shapes (e.g., `backoff_schedule` OR `backoff_schedule_seconds`; `tick_iteration_backstop` OR `tick_iteration_backstop_ticks`; `watchdog_hours` OR `watchdog_period_hours`; `cadence_pivot_threshold_pct` OR `cadence_pivot_spend_fraction`) so the artefact author can pick whichever shape reads best — the strict equality check (or fraction-percent normalisation, for the cadence-pivot lint) is the gate, not the field name. Default expected values are locked by paired tests so a silent edit to a constant surfaces loudly (rule-#10 ratchet — drift requires a Pivot, not a one-line PR). Dormant state (rule #7 — graceful degrade): missing `config/<file>.json` exits 0 with a stderr advisory and re-arms the moment the artefact lands — same precedent as `check-mape-k-budget-cap.mjs` and `check-skill-rule-cap.mjs`'s retired-Skill terminal state. Pivot (rule #9, per-lint): if the prose anchor itself moves before the corresponding config artefact lands (e.g., the fixed `5s → 30s → 5min` ladder is replaced by exponential backoff, or the 12 h watchdog is replaced by adaptive cadence per `mape-k-loop`'s monthly self-calibration extending T to 18 h), retire that specific lint and write a new one against the replacement anchor.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Gamma, Helm, Johnson, Vlissides, *Design Patterns*, Addison-Wesley, 1994 (Adapter / Strategy); Aho, Sethi, Ullman, *Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools*, Addison-Wesley, 1986 (recursive-descent parser shape); Beyer, Jones, Petoff, Murphy (eds.), *Site Reliability Engineering*, O'Reilly 2016, Ch. 3 (error-budget framing — token economy is the budget contract); Meszaros, *xUnit Test Patterns*, Addison-Wesley, 2007 (real-implementation contract test against committed fixtures); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every dep behind interface; the adapter wraps the JSONL on-disk format Anthropic writes); rule #1 (vision.md § 1 — push upstream first; `weeklyHeadroomFraction` is `0` in v0 because Maciek's P90 ML predictor is not exposed without their CLI, and a `--json` mode is the upstream feature request); upstream reference: `claude_monitor/data/reader.py` (recursive `.jsonl` glob), `claude_monitor/core/data_processors.py` (`TokenExtractor.extract_tokens` summing `input_tokens + output_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens`), `claude_monitor/data/analyzer.py` (5h `SessionBlock` rounding rule), `claude_monitor/core/plans.py` (`PLAN_LIMITS = { pro: 19_000, max5: 88_000, max20: 220_000, custom: 44_000 }`)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Constructor opts: `{ configDir: string, now?: () => Date, plan?: 'pro' \| 'max5' \| 'max20' \| 'custom' }`; `now` defaults to `() => new Date()`, `plan` defaults to `'max5'` (the most common Anthropic tier). `PLAN_CAPS` is exported as a named constant (rule #4 — every magic number named) and locked by paired tests. `snapshot()` rglobs `<configDir>/projects/**/*.jsonl`, stream-parses line-by-line with `JSON.parse` (skip blanks; tolerate malformed JSON per rule #7 graceful-degrade), filters to entries with `message.usage` and positive token sum, dedups by `(message.id, requestId)` (Maciek's rule when the same message appears in two files — resumed sessions write a fresh JSONL), groups sorted entries into 5h SessionBlocks (`block.start = floor(first-entry.ts, hour)` UTC; new block when `entry.ts >= block.end_time` OR `gap-since-last-entry >= 5h`), picks the active block (`now ∈ [start, end)`), sums `input + output + cache_creation + cache_read`, returns `{ tokensRemainingInWindow: max(0, planCap − used), windowSizeTokens: planCap, secondsUntilWindowReset, weeklyHeadroomFraction: 0, observedAt }`. Cold-start path (missing `configDir`, no JSONL files, all-malformed) returns the full plan cap as remaining. **Path correction (rule #9 pivot fired)**: prior brief said `~/.config/claude/`; that env-var path (`CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`) only governs Maciek's *own* settings cache. The actual data source is `~/.claude/projects/<cwd>/<session>.jsonl` — Claude Code writes one JSONL file per session (filename = session UUID), grouped under one directory per cwd (directory name = cwd path with `/` → `-`). 5 committed synthetic fixtures (`single-block`, `two-blocks`, `empty`, `malformed`, `dedup`) under `novel/adapters/token-monitor/test/fixtures/<scenario>/projects/-fixture-cwd/`; each scenario carries a sibling `expected.json` documenting the expected snapshot. **No real `~/.claude/projects/` data is committed.** (vision.md § 7 — graceful-degrade dormant short-circuit when no `--omc-checkout=` is supplied OR no `.omc/state/team/` subdir exists); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — pre-registered Pivot: a non-whitespace                         | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Pure functions `roundTripOmcTask(taskJson) => { ok, diff? }`, `findOmcTaskFiles(omcCheckout) => string[]`, and `parseOmcCheckoutFlag(argv) => string \| null` are referentially transparent — no I/O, no shared state. The CLI (`main()`) is the I/O boundary: parses `--omc-checkout=<path>`, walks `<path>/.omc/state/team/<teamName>/tasks/*.json`, runs `roundTripOmcTask` on each. Whitespace normalisation strips per-line trailing whitespace and collapses trailing newlines so the `JSON.stringify-indent-2` output is comparable to the OMC on-disk bytes; leading whitespace (indentation) is *not* stripped — a non-canonical indent (e.g., 4-space) is a structural fail (the load-bearing pivot trigger). Dormant state (rule #7): missing `--omc-checkout=` OR `<path>/.omc/state/team/` not a directory → exit 0 with stderr advisory; same precedent as `check-mape-k-budget-cap.mjs`'s dormant-config short-circuit. Test coverage (12 cases in `scripts/omc-roundtrip.test.mjs`): synthetic task round-trips cleanly, whitespace-only trailing diff is OK, non-canonical indent fails with a divergence diff, invalid JSON fails with parse-error diff, non-object top-level fails with shape-error diff, non-string input fails fast (rule-#6 let-it-crash on caller contract violation), `findOmcTaskFiles` walks multi-team directories + ignores non-JSON neighbours + returns empty on missing dir, `parseOmcCheckoutFlag` handles absent / present / empty-value cases. Synthetic fixtures only — no real `~/.claude/` or third-party data committed. closes `omc-tasksmd-bridge-v0`'s "Round-trip preserves all task fields" Acceptance through the documented lossy projection — the v0 surface field-mapping is in `novel/bridges/omc-tasksmd/README.md` § "Lossy projection") + `novel/bridges/README.md` (namespace home, intentionally separate from `novel/adapters/` because a *bridge* is a peer-to-peer format pair we own, not an external dep behind an interface)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Read-only adapter bridge between two task-list formats — Helland 2007 (eventual consistency, only the read direction in v0 because OMC's optimistic-concurrency `version` field needs a CRDT story before reverse-sync is safe — filed as `omc-tasksmd-bridge-v1-watcher` in TASKS.md); Hewitt 1973 actor model (TASKS.md is the message store, each emitted block is one message addressed to whoever picks it next); pure-function projection + section-replace idempotency (Knuth 1984 — referential transparency means re-running yields byte-equal output, the load-bearing property for a bridge that may run on a v1+ watch loop); the read-side I/O boundary is a single `fs/promises.readdir` + `readFile` pair (Martin 2017 *Clean Architecture* — I/O at the edge, mapper + sync are pure)                                                               | Helland, P., "Life beyond Distributed Transactions: an Apostate's Opinion", *CIDR* 2007 (the load-bearing pattern: a bridge between two stores converges through eventual consistency; v0 ships only the read direction because that's the safe half until the write-side has a CRDT story); Hewitt, Bishop, Steiger, "A Universal Modular Actor Formalism for Artificial Intelligence", *IJCAI* 1973 (TASKS.md as the message store; each emitted block is one message); Gamma, Helm, Johnson, Vlissides, *Design Patterns*, Addison-Wesley, 1994 (Adapter / Bridge — the namespace-level pattern, distinct from rule #2's *dependency* adapters); Aho, Sethi, Ullman, *Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools*, Addison-Wesley, 1986 (parser shape — `JSON.parse` is the only parser; the format is the round-trip-parseable verdict from `scripts/omc-roundtrip.mjs` per row 61); Martin, R. C., *Clean Architecture*, Pearson, 2017 (I/O at the edge — `OmcReader.list` is the only filesystem touch; mapper + sync are pure); Munafò et al., *Nature Human Behaviour* 1, 0021, 2017 (rule-#9 pre-registration — the lossy-projection pivot was declared in `EXPERIMENT.yaml` *before* code so a `metadata: { … }` field that doesn't fit the documented projection cannot retroactively redefine "lossless"; v0 ships *with* the lossy note rather than expanding scope); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every dep behind interface; OMC's task store is exactly that, behind `OmcReader.list`); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash; the v1+ `merge-by-id` mode throws with a `v1+` message so the caller decides next step rather than the bridge silently doing the wrong thing); rule #7 (vision.md § 7 — graceful-degrade on missing `.omc/state/team/`, on malformed JSON, and on unknown `teamName` filter); research.md § "OMC handoff persistence" (the parseable verdict that unblocked v0); TASKS.md `omc-tasksmd-bridge-v1-watcher` (the next-tier follow-up: chokidar / `fs.watch` + reverse-sync once the CRDT story lands)
