                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | partial                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | **Lossy-projection v0 (declared deviation, pre-registered).** v0 surfaces the documented subset — `id`, `subject`, `description`, `status`, `owner ?? claim.owner`, `blocked_by` (comma-space-joined), `created_at`, `version` — and drops `claim.token`, `claim.leased_until`, `blocks`, `depends_on`, `result`, `error`, `requires_code_change`, `metadata`. The drop is *intentional* (the OMC fields are out of v0 scope; surfacing them in tasks.md would require either widening the tasks.md spec or shipping a second projection mode), and is the load-bearing reason conformance is `partial` rather than `full`. The pivot was pre-declared in `EXPERIMENT.yaml`'s `pivot:` block — "if any field can't be losslessly mapped, document the lossy projection in `novel/bridges/omc-tasksmd/README.md` and ship anyway with a 'lossy v0' note" — so the deviation cannot be retroactively explained away (Munafò 2017 pre-registration). 17 paired tests under `novel/bridges/omc-tasksmd/src/{reader,mapper,sync}.test.ts` cover: cold-start (missing `.omc/state/team/`), malformed JSON skip with stderr advisory, unknown `teamName` filter, multi-team walk (alphabetical), minimum-fields and full-record mapping, `[x]` vs `[ ]` checkbox, `owner ?? claim.owner` precedence, idempotent re-run (byte-equal output), preservation of non-OMC sections, replace-in-place over a stale section (no duplicates), empty-list section, and the `merge-by-id` v1+ throw. Synthetic fixtures only under `novel/bridges/omc-tasksmd/test/fixtures/` — `task-001.json` (full record with claim + metadata), `task-002.json` (completed status + completed*at), `malformed.json` (intentionally invalid JSON for the rule-#7 graceful-skip chaos test, biome-excluded so the linter doesn't try to parse it), and `other-team/task-otr-001.json` (multi-team walk fixture). **No real OMC state from a live install is committed.** Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (no function exceeds 8). All exported functions/methods carry `@otel bridges.omc-tasksmd.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4; helper / type-guard / coercion helpers are `@otel-exempt` with reason. Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6, with three `// rule-6: handled-locally — graceful-skip …` opt-outs at the file-read / JSON-parse / readDir boundaries. The reader is the \_only* I/O boundary; mapper + sync are pure (no clock dependency, no shared state). v1+ direction (`omc-tasksmd-bridge-v1-watcher`, P3 in TASKS.md): chokidar / `fs.watch` reverse-sync after a CRDT story for OMC's `version` field.                                                                                                                                                                                                     | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **No binary bundled in-tree** — `install-openobserve.sh` downloads OpenObserve `v0.80.1` (pinned) from the upstream GitHub release URL at install time. Platform detection covers `darwin-arm64` / `darwin-amd64` / `linux-amd64` / `linux-arm64`; unsupported platform exits non-zero with a clear error. Binary lands at `${HOME}/.local/bin/openobserve`; data dir at `${HOME}/.openobserve/data`. The launchd plist + systemd unit both use `${HOME}` as their only placeholder (consistent with `distribution/launchd/`'s `${MINSKY_HOME}` precedent — but OpenObserve's daemon doesn't need `MINSKY_HOME` because it's a standalone receiver). **Read-only Strategy by construction** — `queryOpenObservePromql` only issues HTTP GETs against `/api/<org>/prometheus/api/v1/query`; there is no POST / PUT / DELETE code path in the dashboard side. The OTLP write side is handled by `@minsky/observability` via the official OTLP HTTP exporters, never by the dashboard. **18 paired Strategy tests** in `novel/dashboard-web/test/strategy.test.ts` cover: snapshot lookup (existing — 3), constant lookup (existing — 1), Prometheus instant-query parser (5: scalar, vector, error status, malformed JSON, empty vector), `queryOpenObservePromql` (5: GET-shape, basic auth, non-2xx, network failure, trailing-slash normalisation), `fetchOpenObserveSnapshot` (3: fan-out, partial failure, format hook), `openObserveGetValue` end-to-end (1: async pre-fetch + sync render-time lookup). **9 OTEL adapter tests** in `novel/adapters/observability/src/otel.test.ts` cover: existing self-test (5), `endpoint` opt flow (1) + trailing-slash normalisation (1) + undefined-endpoint env-fallback (1) + constructor-doesn't-throw with endpoint (1). **Integration test gated** on `OPENOBSERVE_INTEGRATION=1` env var — skipped in CI by default; runs locally when the user has installed OpenObserve. **Install script syntax-validated** via `bash -n distribution/install-openobserve.sh` (the load-bearing CI gate per rule #10 — no actual install runs in CI). All exported functions carry `@otel <verb>` JSDoc per rule #4; pure helpers (`stripTrailingSlash`, `btoaCompat`, `parsePromqlSuccessData`, `extractScalarValue`, `extractVectorFirstValue`, `resolveOtlpEndpoints`) are `@otel-exempt` with reason. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (the parser is split into 4 helper functions). Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6, with two `// rule-6: handled-locally — graceful-degrade …` opt-outs at the network and JSON-parse boundaries. **Resilience-scout follow-ups (≤2 per the scout cap)**: (a) the metric *names* in `OPENOBSERVE_PROMQL_BY_METRIC_ID` (`token_count`, `supervisor_restart_to_claim_latency_seconds`, `http_get_total{path="/watch.json"}`, `claude_code_api_errors_total`) are the exact strings vision.md publishes — if `@minsky/observability` ever emits under different names, the mismatch is filed as `otel-emission-name-audit` (P3 follow-up); (b) the OpenObserve binary URL pattern (`releases/download/${VERSION}/openobserve-${VERSION}-${target}.tar.gz`) may change — if the quarterly review fires the pivot, fall back to VictoriaMetrics triad per `research.md` PR #43 (the runner-up, MetricsQL is a near-superset of PromQL — migration cost is low). | 73  | `experiments/` directory + `scripts/check-no-singleton-experiment.mjs` (`experiments-directory-migration` — moves the rule-#9 pre-registration substrate from a singleton `EXPERIMENT.yaml` at the repo root to plural `experiments/<id>.yaml` files, one per pre-registered experiment; backwards-compatible at the per-file CLI boundary — every lint still accepts an explicit path; new directory-walker default for the three rule-#9 lints; a one-way ratchet `check-no-singleton-experiment.mjs` asserts the singleton stays gone after the migration; CI workflows `ci.yml` + `experiment.yml` updated to walk the directory)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Pre-registration record (Munafò et al. 2017 — one record per pre-registration, the singular shape AsPredicted enforces) + parametric path root (Hewitt 1973 — the directory is the substrate; making the root parametric via the lint's CLI argument lets the same lint run against minsky's own `experiments/` and against a host's `${MINSKY_HOST_ROOT}/.minsky/experiments/` once `host-root-resolver-prep` lands) + one-way ratchet (rule #10 — when an advisory or transitional state is replaced by a new structural answer, the old state cannot quietly come back); concurrent-experiments support (Fagerholm et al. RCoSE 2014 — the per-change runner must support multiple A/B tests in flight)                                                                                                                                                          | Munafò, M. R., Nosek, B. A., Bishop, D. V. M., et al., "A manifesto for reproducible science", *Nature Human Behaviour* 1, 0021, 2017 (the singular-record-per-pre-registration shape — `experiments/<id>.yaml` matches the AsPredicted source pattern; the singleton was a Minsky-coined deviation this migration retires); Fagerholm, F., Guinea, A. S., Mäenpää, H., Münch, J., "Building Blocks for Continuous Experimentation", *RCoSE* 2014 (the per-change experiment runner is the first building block — and it must support plural concurrent experiments per the paper's "multiple A/B tests in flight" design); Beck, K., *Extreme Programming Explained*, Addison-Wesley, 1999, Ch. 17 (CI as the constraint enforcer — the directory-walker is the smallest deterministic substrate that supports plural experiments); Hewitt, C., "A Universal Modular ACTOR Formalism for Artificial Intelligence", *IJCAI* 1973 (parametric path root — the substrate boundary is the actor's interface; making the experiments root parametric is the precondition for cross-repo support landing in `host-root-resolver-prep`); rule #1 (vision.md § 1 — don't reinvent: the data layer at `experiment-store/<id>.jsonl` is already plural; the migration matches the pre-registration layer to it, not building a new substrate); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — the migration itself is pre-registered in `experiments/experiments-directory-migration.yaml`; the eat-own-dogfood test); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; the singleton-ratchet `check-no-singleton-experiment.mjs` is the structural one-way guarantee that the singleton stays gone); TASKS.md `experiments-directory-migration` (the parent task this row closes) + `cross-repo-runner-v0` / `host-root-resolver-prep` (the consumers — both are now unblocked because the per-task substrate is plural); user-stories/006-runner-on-any-repo.md (the cross-repo runner's umbrella experiment record graduates to `experiments/006-runner-on-any-repo.yaml` per this migration).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Backward-compatible at the file CLI; new directory default at the no-arg CLI.** The three rule-#9 lints (`check-anchor-primary-source.mjs`, `check-measurement-inspects-output.mjs`, `check-pivot-success-margin.mjs`) each export a new `mainDirectory(directoryPath)` that walks `experiments/*.yaml` (and `*.yml`) sorted alphabetically, runs the per-file `main(file)` on each, and aggregates exit codes (max wins — any single non-zero fails the run). The CLI default switches from `EXPERIMENT.yaml` to `experiments/`; explicit-path invocations (`node scripts/check-X.mjs path/to/file.yaml`) still work for explicit single-file linting. The new ratchet `scripts/check-no-singleton-experiment.mjs` is a pure check (no LLM, no opt-out): exits 1 if `EXPERIMENT.yaml` exists at the repo root, 0 otherwise. The CI workflow `ci.yml` adds a `no-singleton-experiment` job following the `skill-rule-cap` pattern (runs on every event; required check via `needs:`); existing lint job invocations drop the explicit `EXPERIMENT.yaml` arg so they default to walking the directory. The `experiment.yml` workflow's `gate` and `record` jobs now loop over `experiments/*.yaml` (and a defensive `EXPERIMENT.yaml` if the singleton sneaks back, surfacing the regression at the gate before the ratchet). 18 paired tests ship with the migration: 5 walker tests per lint × 3 lints (15) plus 3 ratchet tests = 18 new vitest cases on top of the pre-existing suites. The migrated `experiments/readme-global-install-claim-fix-2026-05-04.yaml` is the first plural file; `experiments/experiments-directory-migration.yaml` is the migration's own pre-registration (the eat-own-dogfood test). The pivot path — per-experiment-id PR-diff scope when a single bad file gates ≥3 unrelated PRs over 14 days — is recorded on the migration's own EXPERIMENT.yaml so the walker's blast-radius failure mode has a structural escape valve. The `novel/experiment-record/spec.md` § "File location" is rewritten to document the plural shape; the singleton mention is replaced. Cross-repo runs (cross-repo-runner-v0, blocked-by this) consume this substrate as `${MINSKY_HOST_ROOT}/.minsky/experiments/<id>.yaml` once `host-root-resolver-prep` parametrises the lint's root. the three rule-#9 substrate lints now resolve their `experiments/` root through the helper; behaviour-preserving for minsky-on-itself — the helper returns `repoRoot` when the env var is unset; cross-repo invocations point at a host's `.minsky/` sidecar where the layout mirrors the minsky repo root)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Parametric path root (Hewitt 1973 — the substrate boundary is the actor's interface; making the experiments root parametric via an env var lets the same lint live in many filesystems) + dependency injection at the edge (Martin 2017 — the helper is a one-line override; consumers call once at module init; the rest of each script is unchanged) + preparation-PR pattern (rule #9 — when the metric source needs to exist before the artefact that depends on it can be built, ship the substrate first)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Hewitt, C., "A Universal Modular ACTOR Formalism for Artificial Intelligence", *IJCAI* 1973 (the substrate boundary is the actor's interface; making it parametric is the precondition for cross-repo lint reuse); Martin, R. C., *Clean Architecture*, Pearson, 2017 (DI at the edge — `getHostRoot` is the substrate-root injection point, the only env-var read in the lint); Helland, P., "Life beyond Distributed Transactions: an Apostate's Opinion", *CIDR* 2007 (the substrate-locality boundary defines the consistency boundary; making it explicit is the precondition for cross-system semantics); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every external dep through an interface; here the "external dep" is the *filesystem root*, made explicit via the helper); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — preparation-PR pattern: the helper lands before the cross-repo runner that consumes it, so the runner can ship as a *small* PR rather than co-shipping the substrate refactor); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; the helper is a pure function over `process.env.MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` + the supplied `repoRoot`, no LLM in the chain); user-stories/006-runner-on-any-repo.md (the umbrella story this helper enables); docs/cross-repo-portability.md (the operative classification — the three rule-#9 lints port into the cross-repo-portable bucket via this helper); rows 71 + 73 (the experiments-directory-migration this helper extends — same substrate, parametric root); TASKS.md `host-root-resolver-prep` (the parent task this row closes) + `minsky-sidecar-bootstrap` / `cross-repo-runner-v0` (the consumers — both unblocked once this row ships).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Behaviour-preserving for minsky-on-itself; new cross-repo path is opt-in via env.** `getHostRoot(repoRoot, envOverride?)` reads `process.env.MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` (or the optional injected env for tests). Returns the env value when set + non-empty (resolved to absolute), otherwise the supplied `repoRoot` (also resolved). 8 paired tests in `scripts/lib/host-root.test.mjs` cover: unset → repo, empty → repo, set → override, relative override resolution, relative repo fallback resolution, env wins over repo, default `process.env` lookup, defensive type-guard for malformed envs. The three rule-#9 substrate lints (`anchor-primary-source` / `measurement-inspects-output` / `pivot-success-margin`) each replace their `resolve(REPO_ROOT, "experiments")` with `resolve(getHostRoot(REPO_ROOT), "experiments")`. Smoke-tested cross-repo: `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT=/tmp/<fixture> node scripts/check-X.mjs` walks the fixture's `experiments/` and exits 0 when the records are valid; same shell command with the env var unset walks minsky's own `experiments/` (the existing two records). 372 vitest cases pass (8 new + the existing 364). Typecheck clean. Biome clean. The helper is the smallest possible carve-out — three lines of substantive logic — so the consumer-side change in each lint is one import + one constant rebinding. The remaining 9 rule lints (rule-1, rule-2, rule-3, rule-4, rule-5, rule-7, pattern-index, skill-rule-cap, mape-k-\* family) deliberately do NOT take the env var in this PR — they ship cross-repo only when the host's source taxonomy enters the constitutional gate (per `docs/cross-repo-portability.md`'s classification, that's deferred to a follow-up step or skipped by-design for rule-1). The deferred lints are not blockers for the cross-repo runner v0 because their substrate is host-source-tree, not host-experiments. Pivot path: if the env var proves brittle in a host repo (e.g., the operator's shell strips env vars between minsky CLI invocation and child-process spawn), pivot to a CLI flag (`--host-root <path>`) on each lint as a v1 follow-up; the helper's signature already accepts an `envOverride` parameter, so the CLI wrapper can synthesize an env from the flag without touching the helper. | 75  | `novel/sidecar-bootstrap/` (`@minsky/sidecar-bootstrap` v0 — `minsky bootstrap <host-dir>` CLI that materialises a per-host gitignored `.minsky/` sidecar so the cross-repo-runner can govern any host repo; pure planner / inferer / diagnoser / schema in `src/` + thin CLI executor in `bin/minsky-bootstrap.mjs`; idempotent re-runs, `--doctor` self-test with green/yellow/red Avizienis lattice, `--repair` mode; mkdir-based concurrency lock at `.minsky/.bootstrap.lock.d` exits 75 on race)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Command pattern (Gamma 1994 — actions are inspectable data the executor walks) + pure-function-with-I/O-at-edge (Martin 2017 — planner / inferer / doctor are referentially transparent; CLI is the only side-effecting layer) + status lattice (Avizienis et al., *IEEE TDSC* 1 (1), 2004 — `aggregateStatus` red dominates yellow dominates green) + idempotent-bootstrap-via-lock (Armstrong 2007 — `setup.sh`'s mkdir-based lock idiom; second invocation exits EX_TEMPFAIL)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Gamma, Helm, Johnson, Vlissides, *Design Patterns*, Addison-Wesley, 1994 (Command pattern — `BootstrapAction` is a discriminated union the executor switches on; testable as data without I/O); Martin, R. C., *Clean Architecture*, Pearson, 2017 (DI at the edge — the `dist/` exports are pure; the CLI binds to `node:fs` once at the top level); Avizienis, A., Laprie, J.-C., Randell, B., Landwehr, C., "Basic Concepts and Taxonomy of Dependable and Secure Computing", *IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing* 1 (1), 2004 (the green/yellow/red lattice of `diagnose()` mirrors the existing `@minsky/adapter-types` `aggregateStatus()`; red dominates yellow dominates green); Armstrong, J., *Programming Erlang*, Pragmatic Bookshelf, 2007 (idempotent supervisor restart — the bootstrap is the host's "start" verb; second invocation must be a no-op, third with `--repair` must restore invariants; the mkdir-based lock at `.bootstrap.lock.d` mirrors `setup.sh`'s atomic-claim idiom and surfaces concurrent races as EX*TEMPFAIL=75 per BSD sysexits.h); Hunt & Thomas, \_The Pragmatic Programmer*, Addison-Wesley, 1999, Tip 8 ("Make It Easy to Reuse" — inference reads existing host signals like `package.json` + `.git/config` rather than re-eliciting); rule #1 (vision.md § 1 — don't reinvent: the bootstrap reuses the host's signals, the YAML is hand-rendered to avoid a yaml dep, the CLI reuses the existing `setup.sh` lock idiom); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every external dep through an interface; the host's filesystem layout IS the dep, made explicit via the `RepoConfig` schema); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash AT the boundary; the planner returns the action list, the executor handles per-action errors with operator-actionable messages, no silent failures); rule #7 (vision.md § 7 — failure-mode discipline; the README's chaos table has 7 rows including bad-input / host-not-fully-onboarded / concurrency / host-config-drift / filesystem-permission / upstream-malformed / external-cleanup); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — pre-registered pivot for the global-ignore mechanism: if `~/.config/git/ignore` proves unwritable on enough hosts, fall back to per-clone `.git/info/exclude`; this pivot is wired into the `RepoConfig.ignore_mechanism` field's three-value enum); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; the bootstrap's behaviour is fully deterministic given typed inputs and a host filesystem state); user-stories/006-runner-on-any-repo.md (the umbrella story this package enables); rows 71 (experiments-directory-migration substrate) + 74 (host-root-resolver — the env var the bootstrap's sidecar plugs into); TASKS.md `minsky-sidecar-bootstrap` (the parent task this row closes) + `cross-repo-runner-v0` (the immediate downstream consumer that reads `.minsky/repo.yaml`).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **No real host filesystem in CI; all I/O at the CLI edge.** 51 paired vitest cases across `src/{schema,inference,plan,doctor}.test.ts` cover: schema (12 tests — happy path × 6, validation failures × 6 covering missing required fields, empty strings, non-string types, invalid enums); inference (15 tests — defaults, git-remote URL parsing for SSH / HTTPS / no-suffix / malformed forms, package.json detection of yarn / pnpm / npm with vs without `lint` script, workspaces detection, default-branch fallback); planner (14 tests — fresh-host all-six-actions emission, idempotent re-run produces only the log-info action, partial-state surfaces only missing artefacts, `ignore_mechanism: per-clone-exclude` and `none` skip the append-to-ignore action, YAML rendering covers all required fields including `null` and empty-overrides cases, round-trip-ability via structural inspection); doctor (10 tests — all-green, each red path individually, yellow on missing global-ignore, red-dominates-yellow aggregation, yellow-dominates-green aggregation, all-green aggregation). Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (no function exceeds 8). All exported functions carry `@otel sidecar-bootstrap.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4 (`parse-repo-config`, `infer-repo-config`, `plan-bootstrap`, `render-repo-yaml`, `diagnose`); helpers (`requireString`, `inferHostRepo`, `inferCommitFormat`, `inferPreCommitCommand`, `inferTicketFormat`, `inferHostPackagesPath`, `quote`) are JSDoc-internal-only. Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6 — the planner has zero try/catch; the executor's `applyAction` switches on the action `kind` and lets per-action errors bubble to the CLI's top-level catch. The CLI's `acquireLock` exits 75 on EEXIST per Erlang/OTP idempotent-supervisor-restart discipline. End-to-end smoke-tested 2026-05-04 against a tmpdir host with a synthetic `package.json` + git remote: bootstrap inferred `host_repo: "test-owner/test-repo"` from `git@github.com:test-owner/test-repo.git`, `pre_commit_command: "yarn lint"` from `packageManager: yarn@4.0.0` + a `lint` script, wrote 5 sidecar artefacts + 1 global-ignore entry, doctor returned GREEN, idempotent re-run was a no-op (only the log-info action). The `--repair` mode currently re-runs the planner (the planner skips already-present artefacts so the result is identical to a normal run); a future enhancement could force-overwrite a corrupted `repo.yaml`, but v0 keeps it identical for simplicity.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | **No real subprocess in CI; v0 ships dry-run as the safe default.** 43 paired vitest cases across 5 files: `task-finder.test.ts` (14 — TASKS.md parsing for sections / ID / tags / details / rule-#9 fields; exact-ID match; case-sensitive ID lookup; title-substring matching for ticket-key form like `AIFN-840`; not-found reporting with available IDs); `experiment-synth.test.ts` (10 — happy-path YAML rendering with multi-line `\|` blocks for hypothesis / anchor + JSON-quoted scalars for success / pivot / measurement; rule-#9 iron-rule violations covering each missing field individually + multi-missing combinations + all-5-missing); `spawn-plan.test.ts` (12 — workingDirectory; branchName from `<branch_prefix><task-id>`; experimentYamlPath at `<host>/.minsky/experiments/<id>.yaml`; `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` / `MINSKY_TASK_ID` / `MINSKY_BRANCH_NAME` env vars; system-prompt overlay references vision.md path + `Hypothesis self-grade` requirement + pre_commit_command branch); `iteration-record.test.ts` (4 — JSONL render shape with trailing newline + valid JSON + null pr_url + 7 verdict variants); `repo-config-loader.test.ts` (3 — flat-YAML parser handling sidecar-bootstrap's render shape including nested-map `lint_substrate_overrides` + comments + the `{}` empty-map sentinel; loadRepoConfig end-to-end happy + missing-required-field paths). Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (every function ≤10 from first writes — the planner / synthesiser / loader were all designed as straight-line code; no helper extractions needed). All exported functions carry `@otel cross-repo-runner.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4 (`load-repo-config`, `parse-flat-yaml`, `find-task`, `parse-tasks-md`, `synthesise-experiment-yaml`, `build-spawn-plan`, `render-iteration-record`). End-to-end smoke-tested 2026-05-04 against a tmpdir host: bootstrap created `.minsky/repo.yaml`; `minsky run aifn-840-slash-command-labels --host <tmpdir>` parsed the task, synthesised the EXPERIMENT.yaml at `.minsky/experiments/aifn-840-slash-command-labels.yaml` (with all 5 rule-#9 fields), wrote the runner plan to stdout (taskId / branchName / env / system-prompt overlay / brief), appended the `verdict: planned` iteration record to `.minsky/experiment-store/cross-repo/aifn-840-slash-command-labels.jsonl`. A separate smoke against a TASKS.md row missing all 5 rule-#9 fields exited 1 with `rule-9 violation: task ... is missing required field(s): Hypothesis, Success, Pivot, Measurement, Anchor` and `Rule #9 is iron — no exemption` — exactly the rule-#9 iron-rule discipline the synthesiser enforces. Conformance is `partial` (not `full`) because v0's `--live` flag is a placeholder — it writes the EXPERIMENT.yaml + plan but doesn't yet spawn Claude Code; the spawn-wiring (importing `@minsky/tick-loop` + `@minsky/budget-guard` and invoking them from the bin) ships in v1 to keep v0 small and reviewable. The seam is already in the system-prompt overlay + brief that the bin produces; v1 just adds the spawn call. The `cross-repo-runner-aifn-840-integration-test` step ships AIFN-840 against a real iep-capabilities host — that's the v0 acceptance for the umbrella user-story-006 metric (`cross_repo_runs_validated_pct ≥ 80%`). `cross-repo-runner-aifn-840-integration-test` (the parent task this row closes) + `cross-repo-ci-action` (the next-step downstream consumer that runs the test against host PRs via the GitHub API).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **9 paired vitest cases run against tmpdir hosts via the real `minsky-bootstrap.mjs` + `minsky-run.mjs` CLIs.** Each test creates a tmpdir, initialises it as a git repo with a synthetic remote URL (`git@github.com:test-org/test-iep-capabilities.git`) + a `package.json` with `lint` script + `packageManager: "yarn@4.0.0"`, writes an AIFN-840-shaped `TASKS.md` row, runs the bootstrap CLI under an isolated `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` so the test's global-ignore writes don't pollute the operator's real config, runs the runner CLI, asserts the resulting filesystem state. The 9 cases cover: (1) bootstrap creates `.minsky/repo.yaml` + `.minsky/experiments/` + `.minsky/vision.md` symlink with inferred values (`host_repo: "test-org/test-iep-capabilities"`, `pre_commit_command: "yarn lint"`); (2) runner dry-run synthesises the EXPERIMENT.yaml at `.minsky/experiments/<id>.yaml` with all 5 rule-#9 fields rendered correctly (id, hypothesis, success, pivot, measurement, anchor); (3) runner appends iteration record to `.minsky/experiment-store/cross-repo/<id>.jsonl` with `verdict: planned`, `host_repo`, `branch: feat/<task-id>`, JSON-parseable shape; (4) runner emits the RunnerPlan to stdout with taskId, branchName, MINSKY_HOST_ROOT env, system-prompt overlay containing "Hypothesis self-grade" and the host's `pre_commit_command`; (5) ticket-key form (`AIFN-840` query) finds the task by title-substring match (case-insensitive); (6) idempotent re-run appends a second iteration record without crashing (both records have the right shape); (7) rule-#9 violation (task missing all 5 fields) exits 1 with `rule-9 violation`, all 5 missing field names listed, `iron` in the message; (8) not-bootstrapped run (no prior `minsky-bootstrap`) exits 1 with `not bootstrapped` + the bootstrap suggestion; (9) task-not-found exits 1 with `task "no-such-task" not found` + the available-IDs list. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (the test file is straight-line `describe` / `test` calls + small helpers `createFixtureHost`, `cleanupFixture`, `runBootstrap`, `runMinskyRun`, `runNode`). The runbook at `docs/cross-repo-runner-aifn-840-runbook.md` documents the operator-driven workflow for the live AIFN-840 fix (the v0 spawn path is operator-driven; v1 will auto-spawn). The runbook's Step 4 documents the manual iteration-record append for v0 — v1 will detect the merged PR via `gh pr view` and write `verdict: validated` or `regressed` automatically. End-to-end smoke 2026-05-04: `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/integration` exits 0 with `Tests 9 passed (9)`. The test file pattern is the inaugural example for the cross-repo-runner ecosystem; future host-specific integration tests (e.g., agentbrew, taskgrind, bosun) will mirror this shape — `<host-name>-shape.test.ts` under `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/integration/`. | 78  | `scripts/check-rule-11-no-flaky-gates.mjs` + `scripts/check-rule-11-no-flaky-gates.test.mjs` + `test/fixtures/rule-11-flake-detection/` + `tmp/rule-11/.gitkeep` + `docs/rule-11-flake-detection.md` + `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (`rule-11-flake-detection-lint` — auto-detect ratchet for rule #11; pure `detectFlakeRate(runs)` decision function over GitHub workflow runs identifies same-SHA failure→success pairs (Memon 2017 § 3, DETECTOR), surfaces a `FlakeReport` when per-job rate ≥ 10% AND ≥5 same-SHA pairs (DORA test-reliability mark, Forsgren *Accelerate* 2018 Ch. 3); CLI shim ingests via `--workflows ci` (live `gh run list`, 30s timeout) or `--fixture <path>` (hermetic JSON); `--emit <dir>` writes a TASKS.md task-block draft + a workflow-patch instruction file per surfaced flake, idempotent re-emit, lighthouse-mobile downgrade #125 as canonical example; CI job runs on pull_request with `continue-on-error: true` and uploads `tmp/rule-11/*` as workflow artefacts)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Pure-function-with-I/O-at-edge (Martin 2017 — `detectFlakeRate` + `renderTaskBlock` + `renderWorkflowPatch` are referentially transparent; the operator merges it; never auto-mutate CI config; rule #6 — let-it-crash, the human is the supervisor)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Memon, A., Gao, Z., Nguyen, B., et al., "Taming Google-Scale Continuous Testing", *ICSE-SEIP* 2017 (the per-job same-SHA pair primitive — DETECTOR uses 10% as the empirical "team learns to ignore" cut-off); Forsgren, N., Humble, J., Kim, G., *Accelerate*, IT Revolution Press, 2018, Ch. 3 (DORA test-reliability — flaky gates teach the team to ignore failure; the 10% threshold is DORA's mark); Martin, R. C., *Clean Architecture*, Pearson, 2017, Ch. 22 (pure-function seam at the centre, I/O at the edge — `detectFlakeRate` is the seam, `ingestFromGh` + `emitPatches` are the edges); Beck, K., *Extreme Programming Explained*, Addison-Wesley, 1999, Ch. 17 (CI as the constraint enforcer — the auto-detect ratchet is the constraint; the human-in-the-loop patch review is the enforcer); rule #11 (vision.md § 11 — no flaky metric is a load-bearing claim; this lint is rule #11's mechanical ratchet, the difference between "we say flakes are forbidden" and "we mechanically catch them"); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash; the lint surfaces the patch, never auto-mutates CI config); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; pure decision function over CI run history); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — pre-registered as 4 sub-task experiments at `experiments/rule-11-flake-detection-{pure-fn,cli,patch-emit,ci-wire}-2026-05-04.yaml`; the parent epic was decomposed because operator discipline alone is insufficient — rule #11 needs a structural ratchet); TASKS.md `rule-11-flake-detection-lint` (parent tracker — the 4 sub-tasks `rule-11-flake-detection-pure-fn` / `cli` / `patch-emit` / `ci-wire` close to it); rows 9 + 10 (vision.md success-criteria — DORA test-reliability + token-budget honoring depend on a CI substrate that operators trust, which fails when flaky gates erode that trust).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Decomposed into 4 one-commit-sized sub-tasks (PRs #133/#134/#135/this PR).** PR #133 shipped the pure decision function (9 paired tests covering above-threshold / below / sample-size guard / empty / singleton / regression-not-flake / mixed-job / out-of-order / cross-workflow same-name). PR #134 shipped the CLI shim (`--fixture <path>` for hermetic CI; `--workflows <list>` for live `gh run list` with 30s timeout) + 7 more paired tests + 2 JSON fixtures under `test/fixtures/rule-11-flake-detection/`. PR #135 shipped `emitPatches` + `renderTaskBlock` + `renderWorkflowPatch` + `reportBasename` + `--emit <dir>` flag + 10 more paired tests (idempotency via `writeIfChanged` content compare; multi-report; CLI-emit clean+flaky paths). The parent task's pre-registered Pivot ("if workflow-patch generation proves brittle across YAML formatting variations, pivot to a human-readable instruction file") fired during sub-task 3 — the unified-diff form was rejected pre-implementation in favour of an instruction-file form, robust by construction; the lighthouse-mobile downgrade in #125 used this exact shape. This PR (sub-task 4) wires the `rule-11-flake-detection` CI job into `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: runs on `pull_request` only, `continue-on-error: true` so the detector itself stays informational (paradox-aware — a detector that fails the build on every detection becomes a flaky-feeling gate; per the parent task's Pivot, the lint is detection + scaffolding, not decision-making), shells out to `gh run list --workflows ci` via the runner's `GITHUB_TOKEN` (read access to the same repo's Actions, no extra secret), uploads `tmp/rule-11/*-task-block.md` + `*-workflow.patch` as workflow artefacts via `actions/upload-artifact@v4` with `if-no-files-found: ignore` so the green path stays quiet. `tmp/rule-11/.gitkeep` is tracked; the auto-emitted artefacts are gitignored so accidental local invocations don't pollute the repo. `docs/rule-11-flake-detection.md` documents the lighthouse-mobile downgrade shipped in #125 as the canonical "what success looks like" example so operators reading an auto-emitted patch know what shape merged work takes. End-to-end smoke against the local repo's `ci.yml` history: 26/26 vitest cases pass (9+7+10 from sub-tasks 1+2+3); typecheck + biome clean; `tasks-lint TASKS.md` clean. The full chain from "lint surfaces a flake" to "operator merges a fix" is now mechanical: detect → emit → review → merge. Rule #11 step 5 (auto-detect) is satisfied; the operator-discipline-only loop becomes a structural CI invariant. Pure helpers exposed for tests: `parseCliArgs`, `decodeContentsResponse`, `synthesiseVerdict`, `runPortableLints`, `runGh`. the production measurement; v0's measurement is the local self-test). Tests cover all four failure-mode-table rows from TASKS.md `cross-repo-ci-action` plus the happy path: (1) `happy` → success; (2) `missing-self-grade` → failure with `pr-self-grade` row red; (3) `bad-anchor` (Medium URL) → failure with `anchor-primary-source` row red; (4) `experiment-unreadable` → experiment-rooted lints skip with reason; (5) `force-pushed` mid-run → neutral with "head SHA mismatch — re-emit dispatch". 15 vitest cases + 8 dispatch-emit cases all green; `node scripts/cross-repo-check-runner.mjs --self-test` exits 0 against the in-repo `gh` stub. The C3 fallback (host-side pre-push hook installed by `minsky bootstrap`) is already shipped, so operators are never blocked on C2 (~95 % coverage from C3 alone). The Pivot threshold (>10 % missing checks over 30 days once live, or sustained dispatch-delivery flakes → retire C2 to C3) is documented in the runbook and in the experiment's `pivot` field. pure `checkRule12ScopeDiscipline({ changedFiles, tasksMd, experimentsByPath, prBody, optOuts })` decision function over the PR diff classifies every newly-added (status A) public artefact under `novel/**`, `scripts/*.{mjs,sh,ts,js}`, `.github/workflows/*.yml`, or `distribution/**` into one of `justified-by-task` / `justified-by-experiment` / `human-approved` / `unjustified`;                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | **Pre-registered in `experiments/scope-discipline-policy-lint-2026-05-04.yaml`.** ≥6 paired vitest cases (currently 14) cover: justified-by-task (TASKS.md task block mentions path), justified-by-experiment (`experiments/*.yaml` body mentions path), unjustified (no resolution), human-approved via in-file comment, human-approved via PR-body comment, modifications + renames grandfathered, test/fixture files skipped, top-level workflow file justified, top-level script unjustified, paths outside eligible roots skipped (docs/, root \*.md, user-stories/), mixed batch (some justified, some not — only the unjustified are flagged), empty diff is a no-op, in-file opt-out wins over PR-body opt-out (more specific), bare path mention without an `**ID**:` block does NOT justify (the lint requires a real task block, not just prose). The CLI accepts `--diff=<file>` (a `<status>\t<path>`-formatted file) for hermetic fixture testing — `test/fixtures/rule-12/new-feature-without-experiment.diff` (negative — no justification → exit 1) and `test/fixtures/rule-12/new-feature-with-experiment.diff` (positive — this PR's own additions resolve via the pre-registered experiment + the `scope-discipline-policy-lint` TASKS.md task block → exit 0) are the two canonical fixtures. The CI job (`rule-12-scope-discipline`) runs on every PR + push, fetches `origin/main` (the diff base), pulls the PR body via `gh pr view`, and runs both the production lint (`node scripts/check-rule-12-scope-discipline.mjs`) and the unit tests (`pnpm exec vitest run scripts/check-rule-12-scope-discipline.test.mjs`); the job is wired into the `ci` aggregator's `needs:` list and into the gate-loop accepting `success` only. The Pivot path documented in the experiment's `pivot` field (status-R awareness for legitimate file moves; manifest approach via `package.json#minsky-public-surface` if substring-matching proves brittle) is the rule-#9 escape valve — false-positive trigger ≥2 / month sustained over a 4-week window. Rule #12 is now mechanical, not discipline-only. | 79  | `setup.sh --dogfood` (`setup-dogfood-flag` — closes the operator-onboarding gap surfaced by the 2026-05-04 user voice brief; consolidates `distribution/README.md`'s multi-line install snippet into a one-command invocation: detects OS via `uname -s`, renders `distribution/{systemd,launchd}/*` templates with `${MINSKY_HOME}` substitution via `envsubst`, drops them into the user-scope unit dir, idempotently loads/reloads the supervisor target, verifies `is-active`, prints operator's tail-logs / pause / status hints; idempotent re-run re-renders templates and re-loads — no-op on already-active supervisor)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Modeless one-step affordance (Cooper *About Face* 2014 ch. on modal-vs-modeless interfaces — the operator's intent is unambiguous from the flag name; a one-command operator action mirrors OTP's `application:start/1` boot semantic per Armstrong 2007) + sidecar config consolidation (Hunt & Thomas, *The Pragmatic Programmer*, 1999, Tip 8 — "Make it easy to reuse"; the install snippet is documented prose that gets copied + edited; the flag is the reusable affordance) + reuse-existing-primitives (rule #1 — the unit-file templates + `lint-units.sh` smoke + the multi-line snippets all already exist; this row is the one-line orchestration)                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Cooper, A., *About Face: The Essentials of Interaction Design*, 4th ed., Wiley, 2014 (modal-vs-modeless — `--dogfood` is modeless: same affordance whether the supervisor is currently loaded or not; reload semantics handled internally); Armstrong, J., *Programming Erlang*, Pragmatic Bookshelf, 2007 (supervision tree — `enable --now` is OTP's start-the-supervisor verb; one-command operator action mirrors OTP's `application:start/1`); Hunt, A., Thomas, D., *The Pragmatic Programmer*, Addison-Wesley, 1999, Tip 8 ("Make it easy to reuse" — copy-paste-with-edits is not reuse; one-command flag is); rule #1 (vision.md § 1 — don't reinvent: the unit-file templates + lint-units smoke + multi-line snippets already exist; the flag is one-line orchestration); rule #12 (vision.md § "Scope discipline" — Minsky-on-itself is the substrate the rule applies to; making the loop *easy to start* is the rule's enabling step); user-stories/001-loop-runs-overnight.md (the umbrella user story for Minsky-on-itself; this row is the operator-side affordance the story's "Story" section assumes is in place); rows 4 (supervision tree) + 67 (tick-loop daemon v0) (the substrates this flag activates).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Single bash block, OS-detected; no new code outside `setup.sh`.** The new `MODE="dogfood"` block in `setup.sh` (after the doctor block) detects OS via `uname -s`, renders the appropriate templates with `MINSKY_HOME=$ROOT envsubst`, drops them in `~/.config/systemd/user/` (Linux) or `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` (macOS), runs `launchctl bootout` then `bootstrap` (idempotent on macOS) or `systemctl --user enable --now minsky-supervisor.target` (Linux), confirms `is-active`, prints next-step hints (tail logs / pause / status / re-run). The `bootout`-then-`bootstrap` idiom is the macOS-side idempotency pattern (`bootstrap` errors if already loaded; `bootout` is a no-op when not loaded). `envsubst` is required (added to `--doctor`'s prereq list as a follow-up if it proves missing on enough hosts). The flag is invoked as `./setup.sh --dogfood` with no args; the multi-line snippet at `distribution/README.md` stays as the under-the-hood reference for operators who need to debug the install (custom unit dir, sandboxed shell). End-to-end smoke 2026-05-04: `bash -n setup.sh` syntax-clean; `./setup.sh --help` shows `--dogfood`; `MINSKY_HOME=$(pwd) envsubst '${MINSKY_HOME}' < distribution/launchd/com.minsky.tick-loop.plist` produces a plist with `<string>$MINSKY_REPO</string>` for `WorkingDirectory`. Pre-registered in `experiments/setup-dogfood-flag-2026-05-04.yaml`. The Pivot path (per-OS install scripts at `distribution/{launchd,systemd}/install.sh` if the bash case-statement boundary proves brittle) is documented in the experiment's pivot field.
